design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate through linear levels, combat hostile NPCs and aliens, solve environmental puzzles, advance narrative through scripted sequences
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Player isolated from squad must survive alien incursion and black operations opposition while uncovering government conspiracy
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience the same catastrophic Black Mesa incident from the perspective of a U.S. Marine soldier rather than scientist; lead and command fellow Marines
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Shifts narrative perspective to show the 'enemy' side of Half-Life; emphasizes squad-based combat and leadership mechanics absent from original; maintains continuous first-person immersion with no camera breaks

difficulty.checkpointing: Chapter-based with continuous progress; environment loads seamlessly between sections
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player occasionally supported by friendly NPCs (security guards, scientists, Marines) who provide fire support, healing, and obstacle removal; reduces solo difficulty burden

progression.upgrade_system: Squad composition system - player can command three Marine types: soldiers (firepower), combat medics (healing), engineers (obstacle removal)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Chapter-based progression through Black Mesa facility with scripted narrative events; new weapons and squad types become available as story progresses
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer deathmatch mode with new environments and weapons
Post-release capture-the-flag mode with additional levels, items, and powerups
Alternative weapon loadouts and squad compositions for different tactical approaches

world_building_techniques: Scripted sequences integrated into continuous first-person gameplay (no cutscenes)
NPC dialogue from security guards and scientists conveying plot information
Environmental storytelling through Black Mesa facility design and alien incursion evidence
Perspective shift narrative - same events as Half-Life shown from Marine unit viewpoint to reveal government conspiracy

faction_indicators: U.S. Marine HECU unit (protagonist faction - soldiers, medics, engineers)
Black Mesa Research Facility personnel (scientist allies)
Race X alien faction (new alien species)
Black operations units (government cleanup crew opposing player)
danger_signals: Alien creature encounters (headcrabs, Vortigaunts, new Race X species)
Black operations operatives hunting to silence witnesses
environmental_storytelling: Black Mesa facility layout showing scientific research infrastructure
Evidence of alien invasion damage throughout environment
Military staging areas and HECU equipment placement
NPC positioning and dialogue revealing chain of command breakdown
